title: rosa afzeliana subsp. subcollina
intro: Rosa afzeliana subsp. subcollina, also known as the Burmese rose or the Myanmar rose, is a species of rose native to Myanmar and northern Thailand. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in its leaves and stems. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. The leaves and stems of Rosa afzeliana subsp. subcollina are typically used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties.
